netOne.title: Full Stack Web Developer
netOne.description:
Migrated ERP system modules from legacy Java architecture to a modern stack using Spring Framework, Angular, and MySQL.
Developed and maintained both backend and frontend functionalities, applying Clean Code principles and producing thorough documentation.
Optimized SQL queries and core algorithms, achieving measurable improvements in performance and scalability.
Redesigned key processes and interfaces to enhance user experience and system efficiency.
Managed tasks autonomously, covering the full development lifecycle from analysis to deployment.
Recently, I’ve been diving into testing for both the backend and frontend, using JUnit with Mockito and Cypress. I find it really rewarding to ensure the system’s quality through proper testing.

hero.title: Java Backend Developer | Software Engineering Student

about.title: About Me
about.paragraph: I'm Nicolás Martín Catania, a 23 years old developer from Argentina, and Software Engineer student, currently working as a full stack developer at NetOne. Beyond my enthusiasm for tech, I enjoy playing the guitar, going to the gym, learning new languages and continuously learning and improving.

project1.name: MySongSet
project1.desc: Web application for organizing songs that I play on the guitar in playlists, using Spotify API integration and a minimalistic UI design, it helped me to not forget about the songs that I know, because I always end up playing the same 10 over and over.

project2.name: Basic products API
project2.desc: API featuring CRUD of different entities such as products and users, user roles, Spring Security, and JWT auth. Includes a basic frontend.

project3.name: Battle simulation in Java
project3.desc: Final project developed in a group for an advanced programming subject. Console application built in Java using many design patterns, with a small connection to Prolog.

project4.name: BudgetKing
project4.desc: Personal finance management app built for practice. Features testing on repository and service layers, and learning about CI/CD pipelines.

project5.name: URL Shortener
project5.desc: Microservices-based URL shortener with hexagonal architecture. Uses RabbitMQ to handle messages and event transmission between microservices, and performs asynchronous email sending when users register. Includes logging and Testcontainers for integration testing. Uses k6 for stress testing. In development.

project6.name: Resilience & Observability Demo
project6.desc: Spring Boot example demonstrating resilience patterns with Resilience4j and observability using HTTP client.

project7.name: The Runner
project7.desc: Virtual threads practice with Spring Boot 4.0.2, using configuration classes to inject executor service.

project8.name: Simple Gym Modulith
project8.desc: Learning project bridging monolith and microservices using Spring Modulith, enforcing domain boundaries and event-driven architecture.

project9.name: The Testcontainers
project9.desc: Learning about testcontainers integration in Spring Boot 4.0.2.

uni.title: Software Engineer student
uni.description: With 50% of the career completed and GPA of 8 out of 10, this career gives me a strong background in computer science, data structures, and algorithms with C, OOP with Java, as well as systems analysis, systems design and basic networking and cryptography knowledge.

ap.title: Fullstack software developer
ap.institution: "Argentina Programa", provided by the National Ministry of Economy
ap.description: First stage completed where we learned OOP with Ruby and JS. On the other hand, I was not able to complete the second stage due to personal issues, which covered Angular, Java, and Spring Boot programming to create RESTful APIs and web applications, although I learned a lot of valuable knowledge.
